title: Proverbs from Harley MS 3362 (Wright's 1854 selection)
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository representation and version of University of Michigan CME00128, which encodes the one-page PROVERBS item printed on p.309 of The Retrospective Review, new series no. VII (May 1854). DIMEV attributes the signed W. item to editor Thomas Wright. The print selects 31 Middle English sayings from British Library Harley MS 3362 and displays them in 33 lines, two of which are indented continuations. The exact continuous manuscript locus is not established by the edition or current public catalog: target-relevant DIMEV records securely identify readings on ff.2v and 4v, while prosified or fragment associations involve ff.4–5 and expose conflicting legacy loci. Complete supporting scans of the 1854 serial carrier and Förster's 1902 comparative article, plus DIMEV v1.0, are cached with fixity. The current British Library record exposes no public digital images; onsite consultation and paid whole-object photography remain request routes.
review_notes: The primary subject is the repository artifact at CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00128.xml. The U-M encoding, Wright's serial item, the bound volume, the Internet Archive scan, Harley MS 3362, and the scholarly catalog records are separate entities.

The cataloged historical unit is the exact 31-saying selection printed in 1854, not all proverbial material in Harley MS 3362 and not one ideal DIMEV work. The 33 XML L elements represent 31 displayed sayings because print lines 8 and 10 are indented continuations of lines 7 and 9.
DIMEV treats some exact Harley readings as verse couplets and others as prose or prosified associations. XML LG/L serialization therefore does not establish that every saying is verse.
The six printed ellipses are preserved, but the available edition and catalog do not establish whether each marks illegibility, physical loss, editorial omission, or an incomplete exemplar.

The current British Library record marks Digitised Content as No and exposes no viewer or IIIF route as of 2026-07-13. This dated negative finding does not prove that no unpublished, internal, or legacy photography exists.
The pinned CME root LICENSE uses Unlicense language framed as software. Its application to corpus XML/data/markup remains unresolved and is not broadened into a high-confidence rights claim.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00128
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00128; the file is 2,912 bytes.
TEMPHEAD records a 2016-08-31 proofing and structure review, not the original encoding date.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00128
entities.description: A keyed electronic transcription of the one-page 1854 PROVERBS item, including its heading, source headnote, 33 print-line units, six ellipses, and closing signature W.

entities.label: Thomas Wright, Proverbs from MS. Harley 3362 (May 1854)
entities.description: The immediate source edition for CME00128: a one-page item in the Anecdota Literaria sequence, headed PROVERBS, introduced as from Harley MS 3362 of the end of the fifteenth century, and signed W. DIMEV's bibliography attributes the item to Thomas Wright as editor.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., 'Anecdota Literaria: Proverbs from Ms. Harley 3362, of the end of the fifteenth century,' The Retrospective Review, new series 2, no. VII (May 1854): 309.
bibliographic.title: Anecdota Literaria: Proverbs from Ms. Harley 3362, of the end of the fifteenth century

notes: The printed page itself signs the item only W.; Thomas Wright is the DIMEV bibliographic attribution and is not a claim of medieval authorship.
The item begins below the preceding contribution on printed p.309 and ends at the page foot; the next item begins on p.310.

entities.label: The Retrospective Review, new series volume II (1854)
entities.description: The bound 1854 volume containing quarterly no. VII (May 1854), pp.209–320, and the target item on p.309. It is a collected serial carrier rather than the immediate editorial item.

bibliographic.citation: The Retrospective Review, new series, vol. II (London: John Russell Smith, 1854).
bibliographic.title: The Retrospective Review

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of The Retrospective Review, new series volumes I–II
entities.description: An 882-page scan combining new-series volumes I (1853) and II (1854). The target is printed p.309 of volume II, PDF page 759 / Internet Archive leaf n758, from the PROVERBS heading below the preceding item through the signed W. at the page foot.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete two-volume PDF with OCR text and provider metadata derivatives

entities.label: Max Förster, Frühmittelenglische Sprichwörter (1902)
entities.description: A comparative study and edition of the bilingual proverbs in Cambridge, Trinity College MS O.2.45. Its notes quote several Harley MS 3362 parallels relevant to CME00128 from John Mitchell Kemble's earlier transcription, using an old foliation system. It is supporting scholarship, not an immediate source of the U-M encoding or Wright's 1854 item.
notes: The Harley parallels cite Kemble and use old folio numbers 3, 5, 6, and 7 for target readings; those numbers are not silently converted into current British Library/DIMEV foliation.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Englische Studien volume 31 (1902)
entities.description: A complete 468-page scan of the journal volume. Förster's article occupies printed pp.1–20, PDF pages 11–30 / Internet Archive leaves n11–n30.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume PDF with OCR, page-number, scandata, and provider metadata derivatives

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 3362
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English collection of macaronic poems, songs, proverbs, riddles, grammatical rules, and courtly lyrics. The opening proverb run begins on f.2r. DIMEV securely places target readings on f.2v and f.4v and associates further target text with ff.4–5, but the exact continuous start and end surfaces of Wright's 31-saying selection remain unresolved without manuscript images or a direct folio-by-folio concordance.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.notes: The 1854 source calls the manuscript end of the fifteenth century; the current catalog supplies only the broader century.

holding.notes: All manuscripts and archives are consulted at the British Library in London; current physical requests require a valid Reader Pass and the exact shelfmark.

physical_description.support: Paper and parchment; f.1 is parchment
physical_description.extent: ff.91, with the catalog rendering the added flyleaf count as '(+ 3 unfoliated paper flyleaves at the beginning + at the end)'; one unfoliated paper pastedown is on the recto of f.[iii]
notes: The current catalog identifies Gothic script and English origin.
The present binding is a post-1600 British Museum in-house binding.
The catalog's broad ff.2r–79v contents range is not the target selection's extent.

entities.label: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record 040-002049193
entities.description: The current official whole-manuscript record for Harley MS 3362, with identifiers, broad contents, date, languages, origin, physical description, binding, provenance, and access route. It does not isolate Wright's complete 31-saying selection and exposes no digitised-content link.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 target-relevant records for Harley MS 3362
entities.description: Versioned scholarly index records mapping several sayings on the printed page to separately circulating items: secure exact matches in records 6052, 109.5, and 6212; prose or prosified associations in records 5532 and 6523; and an overlapping incomplete fragment in record 5230. The release indexes the entire codex more broadly and does not identify the 31-saying selection as one work.
notes: DIMEV's manuscript index has 16 witness entries for Harley MS 3362 across the codex; those entries are not a count of sayings on the target page.
The legacy or commented loci for the prosified field and dog sayings conflict with current ghost-note loci; both conflicts are preserved as open questions.

access.access_method: Exact catalog record and provider search checked for digitised content
notes: The search facet reports Digitised Content: No, the exact record's URL field for digitised content is empty, and no viewer or IIIF link was exposed. This is a dated negative finding, not proof that unpublished or legacy photography does not exist.

access.access_method: Request the exact shelfmark with a valid Reader Pass; consult manuscripts and archives in London
notes: The current catalog requires a valid Reader Pass and advance or onsite request. Item availability and handling restrictions remain subject to staff review.

access.access_method: British Library digitisation-services order form; quote and condition assessment required
access.contact: British Library digitisation-services order form; permissions@bl.uk for reuse permission
access.format: Manuscript photography in approved delivery formats
notes: For whole-object coverage, request complete color reference photography of covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all unfoliated flyleaves and blanks, both sides of every folio 1–91, edges where offered, the final pastedown, and any detached matter.

Map the work separately to the opening proverb run, provisionally ff.2r–5, and require the final supplied inventory to establish exact surfaces. Do not substitute only target-leaf crops for a whole-object facsimile.
The service says manuscripts must be photographed rather than scanned; delivery and reuse terms depend on the exact quotation and permission.

access.format: ZIP containing XML data and license

rights.component: Underlying fifteenth-century Middle English verbal material represented by the target sayings
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions where historical copyright terms have expired
rights.basis: The underlying verbal material is attested in a fifteenth-century manuscript and was published in 1854; no modern intellectual contribution is included in this component.
The item was published in London in 1854 and is in the public domain in the United States by age.
Internet Archive metadata marks the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT in the US; this is a provider status rather than an affirmative open-content license.
The article was published in 1902 and is in the public domain in the United States by age.

rights.component: University of Michigan keyed electronic text and presentation
rights.basis: The current CME collection identifies its public-domain source editions and presents No Copyright–United States framing; this is a provider rights statement, not an open license for every modern layer.

rights.component: Modern XML transcription, structure, and metadata
rights.basis: The pinned repository root LICENSE contains Unlicense text framed as software, but no path-specific statement establishes that it intentionally covers corpus XML/data/markup.
notes: Do not treat this caution about the modern XML layer as restricting the underlying medieval sayings.

rights.component: New British Library manuscript photography and supplied digital files
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and contractual delivery context
rights.basis: The imaging service requires a quotation, preservation assessment, and separate permission for some reuse. No order or license has been obtained for this manuscript.
The versioned release includes a CC BY 4.0 license.

editorial_practices.description: The 1854 item prints 31 sayings selected from Harley MS 3362 rather than the manuscript's complete proverbial, poetic, and macaronic contents.
editorial_practices.source_feature: A broader opening manuscript proverb run and wider codex collection
editorial_practices.representation: One compact page headed PROVERBS, with six unexplained ellipses

editorial_practices.description: The encoding selects the complete PROVERBS item from the larger Retrospective Review volume, including the heading, source note, all display lines, and signature.
editorial_practices.representation: One DIV1 with one LG, 33 L elements, and a CLOSER/SIGNED
notes: The single LG and L elements reproduce display-line structure; they do not prove that every saying is verse.

evidence.summary: Identifies CME00128 and preserves the complete encoded item: printed page number, heading, source headnote, 33 L elements, six ellipses, and signed closer W.

evidence.citation: Reproducible structural and textual audit of CME00128.xml.
evidence.locator: ETS/TEXT element counts and complete line sequence
evidence.summary: Counts 1 DIV1, 1 PB, 1 HEAD, 1 HEADNOTE/P, 1 LG, 33 L, and 1 CLOSER/SIGNED; confirms 6 ellipses and Middle English TEXT/@LANG. Print inspection shows that lines 8 and 10 are indented continuations, so the page contains 31 displayed sayings rather than 33 independently delimited works.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., 'Anecdota Literaria: Proverbs from Ms. Harley 3362, of the end of the fifteenth century,' The Retrospective Review, new series 2, no. VII (May 1854): 309.
evidence.locator: Volume II printed p.309; PDF page 759; IA leaf n758
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the exact printed selection, heading, source statement, readings, line indentation, ellipses, signed W., spatial page boundary, and placement within May 1854 no. VII and the 1854 bound volume.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for retrospectivere04unkngoog, The Retrospective review, volumes 1–2.
evidence.locator: metadata object and PDF/OCR file records
evidence.summary: Supports the scan identity, Harvard contributor, Google source, complete PDF and OCR derivative sizes and provider checksums, public access, and NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT / US provider status. Internal title pages control over conflicting generic metadata dates.
notes: The live endpoint includes dynamic response fields; the cached response is the exact object described by this checksum.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive page-number and scandata derivatives for retrospectivere04unkngoog.
evidence.locator: pageNumber 309, leafNum 758; visual audit of PDF page 759
evidence.summary: The combined scan has two printed pages numbered 309; the target is the second-volume page at leafNum 758 / one-based PDF page 759. Visual inspection establishes that the target begins at the centered PROVERBS heading below the preceding item's closing rule and ends with W. and a rule at the page foot; n759 begins a separate item.
notes: The companion cached scandata file is 294,336 bytes with SHA-256 11df31da55686e26a086ae70adfd851df11cec0ae5ab91cee3149594c8512371.

evidence.locator: Article pp.1–20; target-relevant Harley comparisons on pp.6–9; PDF pages 11–30
evidence.summary: Supporting scholarship quoting target-related Harley readings through Kemble: the tongue saying without a folio on p.6; cat-beard at old fol.6 and cat-would-eat at old fol.7 on p.7; need at old fol.3 and field at old fol.5 on p.8; and dog at old fol.7 on p.9. These old-foliation citations conflict with or do not cleanly map onto current DIMEV loci and are not normalized silently.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for englische-studien_1902_31, Englische Studien volume 31 (1902).
evidence.locator: metadata object and PDF/OCR/page-data file records
evidence.summary: Supports the 1902 volume identity, complete 468-page PDF, OCR, page-number and scandata derivatives, exact direct URLs, sizes, and provider checksums.
notes: The live endpoint includes dynamic response fields; the cached response is the exact object described by this checksum.

evidence.citation: Page-sequence audit of the cached complete Englische Studien volume 31 scan and Internet Archive page data.
evidence.summary: Sequential page data maps printed p.3 to leafNum 13 and p.20 to leafNum 30; direct PDF inspection establishes the article's unrecognized opening p.1 at leafNum 11. The PDF passes qpdf syntax checks and contains 468 pages.
notes: The companion cached scandata file is 500,102 bytes with SHA-256 4892d30b0eb65defb08f5dfdad20d2a8baa4daa626be0a1a74e5ecfcb1e409ef.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00128.
evidence.summary: Supplies the public item identity and stable CME00128 route; automated retrieval encountered a challenge, so exact text and structure were verified in the pinned repository copy.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
evidence.summary: Describes manual keying from public-domain editions, public educational and research access, bulk XML, and the collection's No Copyright–United States rights statement.

evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark, record ID, MDARK, fifteenth-century date, English origin, broad macaronic contents and opening incipit, languages, material, extent, dimensions, script, binding, holding, access request route, and the empty digitised-content field.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts search for Harley MS 3362.

evidence.summary: Reports Digitised Content: No for the exact record and exposes no public viewer or IIIF link as of the access date.
Supplies the current order route and required request details; states that manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned and that delivery depends on handling suitability and a quotation.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: Supports single-page imaging conditions, preservation-suitability review, fees, delivery restrictions, and the need for separate permission for some reuse.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, target-relevant Harley MS 3362 records and Wright1854b bibliography.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml: record-6052, record-109.5, record-6212, record-5532, record-6523, record-5230; data/Manuscripts.xml: BLHar3362; data/Bibliography.xml: Wright1854b
evidence.summary: Supports Thomas Wright's editorial attribution, exact or associated target readings, IMEV/NIMEV identities, secure f.2v and f.4v loci, the incomplete status of the cat-beard saying, prose/prosified classification of the field and dog sayings, the overlapping 3318.8 fragment, manuscript-level Ely dialect localization, and the internal f.4/f.5 and f.5/f.7 locus conflicts.
notes: The v1.0 ZIP integrity check passed and corresponds to release commit 5ad5f592868471b44fc72f6e2f42db419780b42c.
DIMEV's 16 Harley witness-index entries cover the whole manuscript and must not be treated as 16 or 33 works in the printed selection.

open_questions.question: Which exact recto, verso, and column surfaces contain all 31 sayings in Wright's sequence, and did Wright preserve a continuous manuscript run?
next_steps: Inspect complete color images of the opening proverb run and build a line-by-line concordance from the printed incipit through explicit.
Use f.2v and f.4v only for readings DIMEV securely locates there; retain ff.2r–5 as a qualified search range rather than a confirmed continuous extent.
Do not infer that all 33 XML L elements occupy one folio or that the catalog's broad ff.2r–79v contents range is target extent.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply or authorize a complete color digital facsimile of the entire current physical Harley MS 3362, and what exact storage, Git-publication, viewer, print-on-demand, and commercial permissions would govern it?
next_steps: Request the complete current object: covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves and blanks, every recto and verso of fols.1–91, edges where offered, and detached matter.
Map the work separately to the opening target run and retain the supplied image inventory, quotation, correspondence, and exact license.
Do not describe an order as pending or permissions as granted until an actual request receives a response.

open_questions.question: What does each of the six printed ellipses represent in the manuscript and Wright's editorial process?
next_steps: Compare each elliptical item directly with manuscript images and later editions or transcriptions.
Keep DIMEV's explicit incomplete label for 'Wel wot the cat' separate from the five ellipses whose cause is not established.
Do not normalize the dots into manuscript damage, illegibility, or deliberate excision without source evidence.

open_questions.question: How should the target's prosified sayings, overlapping fragments, and conflicting DIMEV legacy loci be reconciled in a future item-level model?
next_steps: Preserve record-5532 and record-6523 as prose/prosified associations rather than active secure verse witnesses.
Preserve record-5230 as an overlapping incomplete fragment of the final saying rather than silently choosing it over record-6523.
Check manuscript images before choosing between f.4 and f.5 for the field saying or f.5 and legacy f.7 for the dog saying.
Do not map the target foot/shoe line to record-6520: DIMEV explicitly says 4079.3 is not in this manuscript.

open_questions.question: When and where was this exact proverb sequence compiled, and does DIMEV's manuscript-level Ely dialect localization apply uniformly to the target run?
next_steps: Keep the current British Library date and English origin at the witness layer.
Treat Ely as a tentative scribal dialect region, not a place of composition or compilation.
Consult manuscript images and current linguistic scholarship before assigning a narrower work origin or date.
